{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Ushihiko Shrine'''|宇志比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

* {'text': '徳島県鳴門市大麻町大谷字山田66', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇志比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q48758332}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= 宇志比古神社 |画像= [[File:Ushihiko shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px|宇志比古神社]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812}}{{jalink|大麻町大谷|Q11442037}}字山田66 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|丹波道主命|Q11368587|宇志比古尊}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}
{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=宇志比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''宇志比古神社'''（うしひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812}}{{jalink|大麻町大谷|Q11442037}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間（{{jalink|1573年|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1593年|Q6772}}）に{{jalink|長宗我部元親|Q1070082}}の乱入により社殿、古記録、社宝を焼失したが、後に再興された。江戸時代には八幡宮と呼ばれ、明治以降に宇志比古神社を称している。 徳島県内で現存する神社本殿では最古にあたり、{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}12年）{{jalink|12月4日|Q2310}}に本殿が国の重要文化財に指定された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|丹波道主命|Q11368587|宇志比古尊}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} * {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|鳴門線|Q861091}}{{jalink|阿波大谷駅|Q4829630}}より徒歩約10分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 宇志比古神社本殿 文化遺産オンライン] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=宇志比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''宇志比古神社'''（うしひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812}}{{jalink|大麻町大谷|Q11442037}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間（{{jalink|1573年|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1593年|Q6772}}）に{{jalink|長宗我部元親|Q1070082}}の乱入により社殿、古記録、社宝を焼失したが、後に再興された。江戸時代には八幡宮と呼ばれ、明治以降に宇志比古神社を称している。 徳島県内で現存する神社本殿では最古にあたり、{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}12年）{{jalink|12月4日|Q2310}}に本殿が国の重要文化財に指定された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|丹波道主命|Q11368587|宇志比古尊}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} * {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|鳴門線|Q861091}}{{jalink|阿波大谷駅|Q4829630}}より徒歩約10分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 宇志比古神社本殿 文化遺産オンライン] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:need translation]]

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''宇志比古神社'''（うしひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|鳴門市|Q460812}}{{jalink|大麻町大谷|Q11442037}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間（{{jalink|1573年|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1593年|Q6772}}）に{{jalink|長宗我部元親|Q1070082}}の乱入により社殿、古記録、社宝を焼失したが、後に再興された。江戸時代には八幡宮と呼ばれ、明治以降に宇志比古神社を称している。 徳島県内で現存する神社本殿では最古にあたり、{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}12年）{{jalink|12月4日|Q2310}}に本殿が国の重要文化財に指定された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|丹波道主命|Q11368587|宇志比古尊}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} * {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|鳴門線|Q861091}}{{jalink|阿波大谷駅|Q4829630}}より徒歩約10分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 宇志比古神社本殿 文化遺産オンライン] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Naruto City|Q460812}}{{jalink|Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037}} Aza Yamada 66 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity= {{jalink|Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|Ushihiko no Mikoto}}

{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |Shrine rank= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Ushihiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Naruto City|Q460812}}{{jalink|Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{jalink|Tenshō era|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1573|Q6678}}–{{jalink|1593|Q6772}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{jalink|Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{jalink|December 4|Q2310}}, {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|Ushihiko no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{jalink|Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|Naruto Line|Q861091}}{{jalink|Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630}}. === References === * “{{jalink|Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 Ushihiko Shrine Main Hall – Cultural Heritage Online] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushihiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Naruto City|Q460812}}{{jalink|Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{jalink|Tenshō era|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1573|Q6678}}–{{jalink|1593|Q6772}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{jalink|Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{jalink|December 4|Q2310}}, {{jalink|2000|Q1985}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|Ushihiko no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{jalink|Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|Naruto Line|Q861091}}{{jalink|Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630}}. === References === * “{{jalink|Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 Ushihiko Shrine Main Hall – Cultural Heritage Online] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushihiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References === * “{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 Ushihiko Shrine Main Hall – Cultural Heritage Online] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushihiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References === * “{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 Ushihiko Shrine Main Hall – Cultural Heritage Online] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:徳島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References === * “{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ushihiko Shrine''' (うしひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Naruto City|Q460812|WD=Q460812|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ōasa-chō Ōtani|Q11442037|WD=Q11442037|1=Ōasa-chō Ōtani}}. === History === The year of foundation is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine buildings, ancient records, and sacred treasures were destroyed by fire due to an incursion by {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, but were later rebuilt. During the Edo period it was called Hachiman-gū, and since the Meiji period it has been known as Ushihiko Shrine. Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References Among extant shrine main halls in Tokushima Prefecture, it is the oldest, and on {{ill|lt=December 4|Q2310|WD=Q2310|1=December 4}}, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12), the main hall was designated an Important Cultural Property of Japan. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamba no Michinushi no Mikoto|Q11368587|WD=Q11368587|lt=Ushihiko no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === Access === * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Naruto Line|Q861091|WD=Q861091|1=Naruto Line}}{{ill|lt=Awa-Ōtani Station|Q4829630|WD=Q4829630|1=Awa-Ōtani Station}}. === References === * “{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/176932 Ushihiko Shrine Main Hall – Cultural Heritage Online] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ushihiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Naruto]] [[Category:Architecture of Naruto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Tokushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]
